mapping.a_element: Z6-II orbifold compactification of the heterotic string
mapping.b_element: Z_6 orbifold compactification of the E_8xE_8 heterotic string

mapping.a_element: SO(10) and E6 local GUT structures
mapping.b_element: SO(10) multiplets (16-plets) for quarks and leptons

mapping.a_element: minimal supersymmetric standard model (MSSM) spectra with exact R parity
mapping.b_element: supersymmetric Standard Model gauge group and matter content

mapping.a_element: vector-like exotics decoupling along D flat directions
mapping.b_element: absence of exotic states at low energies

mapping.a_element: non-trivial Yukawa matrices for 3 families of quarks and leptons
mapping.b_element: top quark Yukawa coupling arising from gauge interactions, with other Yukawa couplings suppressed

brief_justification: Both papers employ the same underlying mechanism of heterotic string compactification on a Z6 orbifold to derive the MSSM, utilizing local GUT structures (SO(10)) to generate Standard Model matter content and address exotic states. The core machinery—orbifold compactification, gauge group breaking, and matter field generation—is identical, with differences only in model-specific details.
